hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: ∞

With a severe stare, you crack the resolve of all but the most composed adversaries.

To resist your influence, a character must spend additional focus equal to your tier.

Agent (Investigator) Prerequisite: Get ’Em Talking talent

Leader (Politico) Prerequisite: Rumormonger talent
hits.name: Baleful
hits.shortDescription: To resist your influence, a character must spend additional focus equal to your tier.

To resist your influence, a character must spend additional focus equal to your tier.

hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: ▶

You know exactly where to strike to leave your foe reeling in pain. Some might not view it as sporting, but it certainly works.

While you have a hand free, spend 1 focus to make an unarmed attack using Thievery against the Cognitive defense of an enemy of your size or smaller, raising the stakes when you do. On a hit, the target also becomes Stunned until the end of their next turn. On a hit or graze, you can also spend an Opportunity to steal one item on the target’s person that they aren’t holding or wielding. The exact item you can steal is up to the GM’s discretion.

Agent (Thief) Prerequisite: Opportunist key talent
hits.name: Cheap Shot
hits.shortDescription: Spend 1 focus to make an unarmed attack with Thievery vs. Cognitive, raising the stakes. On a hit, the target is Stunned.

elementDisplay.value:
Activation: ▶

You know exactly where to strike to leave your foe reeling in pain. Some might not view it as sporting, but it certainly works.

While you have a hand free, spend 1 focus to make an unarmed attack using Thievery against the Cognitive defense of an enemy of your size or smaller, raising the stakes when you do. On a hit, the target also becomes Stunned until the end of their next turn. On a hit or graze, you can also spend an Opportunity to steal one item on the target’s person that they aren’t holding or wielding. The exact item you can steal is up to the GM’s discretion.

Agent (Thief) Prerequisite: Opportunist key talent

shortDescription.value: Spend 1 focus to make an unarmed attack with Thievery vs. Cognitive, raising the stakes. On a hit, the target is Stunned.

hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: 3

You’ve compiled information on your target’s motive and circumstances. Now, by pressing on your target’s desires, weaknesses, or guilt, you can convince them to back down.

Once per scene, spend 3 focus to make a Deduction test against the Cognitive defense of a character you can influence. You gain an advantage on this test if you know the target’s current motivation, and a disadvantage if you don’t.

On a failure, the target gains an advantage on all tests against you until the end of their next turn.

On a success, you influence the target to back down. They stop opposing your immediate aims in this scene and might even leave the scene. A target can resist this influence, but after they do, they gain a disadvantage on all tests until the end of their next turn.

Agent (Investigator) Prerequisite: Deduction 3+; Hardy talent
hits.name: Close the Case
hits.shortDescription: Spend 3 focus to test Deduction vs. Cognitive, gaining an advantage if you know the target’s motivation.

elementDisplay.value:
Activation: 3

You’ve compiled information on your target’s motive and circumstances. Now, by pressing on your target’s desires, weaknesses, or guilt, you can convince them to back down.

Once per scene, spend 3 focus to make a Deduction test against the Cognitive defense of a character you can influence. You gain an advantage on this test if you know the target’s current motivation, and a disadvantage if you don’t.

On a failure, the target gains an advantage on all tests against you until the end of their next turn.

On a success, you influence the target to back down. They stop opposing your immediate aims in this scene and might even leave the scene. A target can resist this influence, but after they do, they gain a disadvantage on all tests until the end of their next turn.

Agent (Investigator) Prerequisite: Deduction 3+; Hardy talent

shortDescription.value: Spend 3 focus to test Deduction vs. Cognitive, gaining an advantage if you know the target’s motivation.

hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: ∞

You remain mindful of your immediate goals without losing sight of your deeper value, keeping you even-keeled amid chaos.

When you acquire this talent, increase your Cognitive and Spiritual defenses by 2.

Agent (Spy) Prerequisite: Sure Outcome talent

Envoy (Diplomat) Prerequisite: Rousing Presence key talent

Scholar (Surgeon) Prerequisite: Emotional Intelligence talent
hits.name: Collected
hits.shortDescription: Increase your Cognitive and Spiritual defenses by 2.

elementDisplay.value:

Envoy (Diplomat) Prerequisite: Rousing Presence key talent

Scholar (Surgeon) Prerequisite: Emotional Intelligence talent

hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: ∞

By spinning vague rumors into popular gossip, you’ve created a false identity to inhabit.

When you acquire this talent, you put the finishing touches on a false identity for yourself. Discuss the details of this identity with your GM, including which communities you use this identity to move through and what its reputation is within those communities.

Additionally, when you acquire this talent, gain a cultural expertise relevant to your false identity’s culture.

Agent (Spy) Prerequisite: Plausible Excuse talent
hits.name: Cover Story
hits.shortDescription: Gain a false identity and a relevant cultural expertise.

elementDisplay.value:
Activation: ∞

When you acquire this talent, you put the finishing touches on a false identity for yourself. Discuss the details of this identity with your GM, including which communities you use this identity to move through and what its reputation is within those communities.

Additionally, when you acquire this talent, gain a cultural expertise relevant to your false identity’s culture.

Agent (Spy) Prerequisite: Plausible Excuse talent

shortDescription.value: Gain a false identity and a relevant cultural expertise.

hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: ★

When you decide to take a risk, you commit fully in pursuit of the greatest reward.

After you use your Opportunist talent, you can reroll the die one additional time. You must use this final result, and if it includes a C, you lose 2 focus.

Agent (Thief) Prerequisite: Risky Behavior talent
hits.name: Double Down
hits.shortDescription: You can reroll again with Opportunist, but on a Complication, you lose 2 focus.

elementDisplay.value:
Activation: ★

When you decide to take a risk, you commit fully in pursuit of the greatest reward.

After you use your Opportunist talent, you can reroll the die one additional time. You must use this final result, and if it includes a C, you lose 2 focus.

Agent (Thief) Prerequisite: Risky Behavior talent

shortDescription.value: You can reroll again with Opportunist, but on a Complication, you lose 2 focus.

hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: ▷

Speaking quickly gets you out of trouble as often as it gets you into it, and you never miss a chance to get a word in edgewise.

Spend 2 focus to gain 2, which you can only use to make spiritual skill tests via the following actions: Use a Skill, Gain Advantage, or an action from any Agent talent.

During a conversation or endeavor, at the GM’s discretion, you can instead use both 2 to make an additional contribution for the round.

Agent (Thief) Prerequisite: Insight 3+; Double Down talent
hits.name: Fast Talker
hits.shortDescription: Spend 2 focus to gain 2 actions for spiritual tests with Use a Skill, Gain Advantage, or an Agent talent.

hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: ∞

Observing clues in your environment, you slowly but surely build a mental model of the mysteries before you.

After you succeed on a cognitive test against another character, you become Focused until the end of your next turn.

Additionally, when you acquire this talent, gain a utility expertise in Legal Codes.

Agent (Investigator) Prerequisite: Insight 2+; Quick Analysis talent
hits.name: Gather Evidence
hits.shortDescription: Gain Legal Codes expertise. When you succeed on a cognitive test against a target, you become Focused.

elementDisplay.value:

Agent (Investigator) Prerequisite: Insight 2+; Quick Analysis talent

shortDescription.value: Gain Legal Codes expertise. When you succeed on a cognitive test against a target, you become Focused.

hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: 2

With a leading question or cutting remark, you goad others into revealing their motives.

Spend 1 focus to make a Deduction test against the Spiritual defense of a character you can influence. On a success, you learn the target’s motivation for being in the scene. Until the end of the scene, when you make a test to leverage this motivation against the target, you can raise the stakes.

Agent (Investigator) Prerequisite: Insight 2+; Opportunist key talent
hits.name: Get ’Em Talking
hits.shortDescription: Spend 1 focus to test Deduction vs. Spiritual to learn the target’s motivation.

Agent (Investigator) Prerequisite: Insight 2+; Opportunist key talent

shortDescription.value: Spend 1 focus to test Deduction vs. Spiritual to learn the target’s motivation.

hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: ∞

You’ve trained your body to endure pain and fatigue, keeping you in the fight until the bitter end.

When you acquire this talent, your maximum and current health increase by 1 per level. This applies to all previous and future levels; for example, if you choose this talent at level 6, retroactively increase your health by 6.

Agent (Investigator) Prerequisite: Baleful talent

Hunter (Archer) Prerequisite: Steady Aim talent

Leader (Champion) Prerequisite: Valiant Intervention talent

Warrior (Soldier) Prerequisite: Combat Training talent
hits.name: Hardy
hits.shortDescription: Gain +1 max health per level (including previous levels).

elementDisplay.value:
Activation: ∞

hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: ★

Your patron’s name opens doors for you, even in unfamiliar places. By invoking their name or network in the right context, you can expand your influence even further.

When you make a test to interact socially in high society, you can spend 2 focus to add an Opportunity to the result.

Additionally, when you acquire this talent, gain a cultural expertise in High Society.

Agent (Spy) Prerequisite: Have a patron who is part of high society; Cover Story talent

Envoy (Diplomat) Prerequisite: Have a patron who is part of high society; Well Dressed talent
hits.name: High Society Contacts
hits.shortDescription: Gain High Society expertise. Spend 2 focus to add an Opportunity to a test to interact in high society.

hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: ★

You know poise is essential to any disguise, and you can change your perceived persona simply by altering your gait and presence.

When you attempt to disguise yourself, you can use Deception instead of Thievery. You can do this even if you don’t have supplies for a physical disguise.

The first character who sees through your disguise, or who you choose to surprise by revealing your identity, becomes Surprised until the end of your next turn.

Agent (Spy) Prerequisite: Deception 3+; Subtle Takedown talent
hits.name: Mercurial Facade
hits.shortDescription: Disguise yourself using Deception without needing physical supplies. The first character to see through your disguise is Surprised.

elementDisplay.value:
Activation: ★

You know poise is essential to any disguise, and you can change your perceived persona simply by altering your gait and presence.

When you attempt to disguise yourself, you can use Deception instead of Thievery. You can do this even if you don’t have supplies for a physical disguise.

The first character who sees through your disguise, or who you choose to surprise by revealing your identity, becomes Surprised until the end of your next turn.

Agent (Spy) Prerequisite: Deception 3+; Subtle Takedown talent

shortDescription.value: Disguise yourself using Deception without needing physical supplies. The first character to see through your disguise is Surprised.

hits.elementDisplay:
Prerequisite: Varies

Activation: ∞

You deliver your swings, punches, and kicks with practiced efficiency.

When you hit with a weapon or unarmed attack, for each ▶ you used on that attack’s action, increase the damage you deal by 1 + your tier. For example, if you’re level 6 and you make an attack that costs 2, each ▶ deals an extra 1 + 2 damage, for a total of 6 damage.
hits.name: Mighty
hits.shortDescription: When you hit with a weapon or unarmed attack, for each ▶ you used on that attack’s action, increase the damage you deal by 1 + your tier.

elementDisplay.value:
Prerequisite: Varies

shortDescription.value: When you hit with a weapon or unarmed attack, for each ▶ you used on that attack’s action, increase the damage you deal by 1 + your tier.

hits.elementDisplay:
Prerequisite: none

Activation: ★

You instinctively sense moments of consequence. In these moments where the scale teeters between disaster and glory, you can push yourself—and your luck—to tip the outcome in your favor.

Once per round, when you roll a plot die, you can reroll that result. The original roll has no effect and you must use the new result.
hits.name: Opportunist
hits.shortDescription: Once per round, you can reroll your plot die.

elementDisplay.value:
Prerequisite: none

Activation: ★

You instinctively sense moments of consequence. In these moments where the scale teeters between disaster and glory, you can push yourself—and your luck—to tip the outcome in your favor.

Once per round, when you roll a plot die, you can reroll that result. The original roll has no effect and you must use the new result.

shortDescription.value: Once per round, you can reroll your plot die.

hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: R

Years of covert operations have taught you to dissemble on the spot, concocting a reasonable excuse for the most incriminating of behavior.

After you are discovered while hiding or otherwise skulking about, you can use this reaction and spend 2 focus to pass yourself off as doing something innocent. You influence each character who can sense you to believe your excuse.

A character can resist this influence, but after they do, they lose additional focus equal to your ranks in Deception.

Additionally, when you acquire this talent, gain a utility expertise in Sleight of Hand.

Agent (Spy) Prerequisite: Deception 1+; Opportunist key talent
hits.name: Plausible Excuse
hits.shortDescription: Gain Sleight of Hand expertise. When discovered skulking, spend 2 focus to feign innocence.

During a conversation or endeavor, at the GM’s discretion, you can instead use both 2 to make an additional contribution for the round.

Agent (Investigator) Prerequisite: Watchful Eye talent

Activation: ★

You know that risk and reward are inseparable traveling companions, and you love seeing where this pair takes you.

Before you make a test, you can spend 1 focus to raise the stakes.

Agent (Thief) Prerequisite: Insight 2+; Opportunist key talent
hits.name: Risky Behavior
hits.shortDescription: Spend 1 focus to raise the stakes on your test.

elementDisplay.value:
Activation: ★

You know that risk and reward are inseparable traveling companions, and you love seeing where this pair takes you.

Before you make a test, you can spend 1 focus to raise the stakes.

Agent (Thief) Prerequisite: Insight 2+; Opportunist key talent

shortDescription.value: Spend 1 focus to raise the stakes on your test.

hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: ▷

You know every con artist’s trick for avoiding comeuppance, and you can slip from sight with ease.

After you Disengage, you can spend 2 focus to make a Thievery test and compare the result against the Cognitive defense of each enemy who can sense you. You gain an advantage on this test if you’re in cover or an area where your target’s primary sense is obscured (such as darkness).

If you succeed against an enemy, you’re hidden as if you’d succeeded on a Stealth test to hide from them.

Agent (Thief) Prerequisite: Thievery 3+; Surefooted talent
hits.name: Shadow Step
hits.shortDescription: After you Disengage, spend 2 focus to test Thievery vs. each enemy’s Cognitive to hide from them. You gain an advantage if in cover or obscured.

elementDisplay.value:
Activation: ▷

You know every con artist’s trick for avoiding comeuppance, and you can slip from sight with ease.

After you Disengage, you can spend 2 focus to make a Thievery test and compare the result against the Cognitive defense of each enemy who can sense you. You gain an advantage on this test if you’re in cover or an area where your target’s primary sense is obscured (such as darkness).

If you succeed against an enemy, you’re hidden as if you’d succeeded on a Stealth test to hide from them.

Agent (Thief) Prerequisite: Thievery 3+; Surefooted talent

shortDescription.value: After you Disengage, spend 2 focus to test Thievery vs. each enemy’s Cognitive to hide from them. You gain an advantage if in cover or obscured.

hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: ∞

When cracking mysteries, you know that “why” can be as important as “who” or “how.” Once you understand motivations, other details easily fall into place.

You gain an advantage on cognitive tests against a character whose motivation you know; you might learn this motivation from the Get’ Em Talking talent, or from other activities such as researching them.

Additionally, after a character whose motivation you know lies to you, you automatically know they’re being deceitful (but not the nature of their lie).

Agent (Investigator) Prerequisite: Deduction 3+; Hardy talent
hits.name: Sleuth’s Instincts
hits.shortDescription: Gain an advantage on cognitive tests against characters whose motivation you know. You know when those characters lie to you.

elementDisplay.value:
Activation: ∞

When cracking mysteries, you know that “why” can be as important as “who” or “how.” Once you understand motivations, other details easily fall into place.

You gain an advantage on cognitive tests against a character whose motivation you know; you might learn this motivation from the Get’ Em Talking talent, or from other activities such as researching them.

Additionally, after a character whose motivation you know lies to you, you automatically know they’re being deceitful (but not the nature of their lie).

Agent (Investigator) Prerequisite: Deduction 3+; Hardy talent

shortDescription.value: Gain an advantage on cognitive tests against characters whose motivation you know. You know when those characters lie to you.

hits.elementDisplay:
Activation: 2

You swiftly strike at your target’s throat, knees, or another vulnerable area. Before the target can cry out, they are silenced.

Make an unarmed attack using Insight against the Cognitive defense of a character who is Surprised, doesn’t sense you, or doesn’t view you as a threat (such as if you’re disguised). The target must be your size or smaller. Raise the stakes on this attack test. On a hit, the target also can’t communicate until the end of their next turn. Also on a hit, you can spend an Opportunity to Grapple them as ▷, automatically succeeding on your test; while Restrained by this grapple, they are also Prone.

Agent (Spy) Prerequisite: Insight 3+; Cover Story talent
hits.name: Subtle Takedown
hits.shortDescription: Make an unarmed attack with Insight vs. an unsuspecting target’s Cognitive, raising the stakes. On a hit, they can’t communicate.

elementDisplay.value:
Activation: 2

You swiftly strike at your target’s throat, knees, or another vulnerable area. Before the target can cry out, they are silenced.

The target must be your size or smaller. Raise the stakes on this attack test. On a hit, the target also can’t communicate until the end of their next turn. Also on a hit, you can spend an Opportunity to Grapple them as ▷, automatically succeeding on your test; while Restrained by this grapple, they are also Prone.

Agent (Spy) Prerequisite: Insight 3+; Cover Story talent

shortDescription.value: Make an unarmed attack with Insight vs. an unsuspecting target’s Cognitive, raising the stakes. On a hit, they can’t communicate.

Activation: ★

The best gamble is one that secretly isn’t a gamble at all. Your insight sometimes reveals that seemingly random events are part of someone’s plan.

When you use your Opportunist talent, instead of rerolling the plot die, you can spend 2 focus to flip it to a specific result. If the die was showing O, flip it to C4. If it was showing any C, flip it to O.

Agent (Spy) Prerequisite: Insight 2+; Opportunist key talent
hits.name: Sure Outcome
hits.shortDescription: When you use Opportunist, spend 2 focus to change an Opportunity to a Complication, or change any Complication to an Opportunity.

elementDisplay.value:
Activation: ★

The best gamble is one that secretly isn’t a gamble at all. Your insight sometimes reveals that seemingly random events are part of someone’s plan.

When you use your Opportunist talent, instead of rerolling the plot die, you can spend 2 focus to flip it to a specific result. If the die was showing O, flip it to C4. If it was showing any C, flip it to O.

Agent (Spy) Prerequisite: Insight 2+; Opportunist key talent

shortDescription.value: When you use Opportunist, spend 2 focus to change an Opportunity to a Complication, or change any Complication to an Opportunity.

hits.elementDisplay:
Prerequisite: Varies

Activation: ∞

You move through treacherous terrain with confidence and speed.

When you acquire this talent, increase your movement rate by 10.

Additionally, before you take damage from dangerous terrain or falling, reduce that damage by 2 × your tier.
hits.name: Surefooted
hits.shortDescription: Increase your movement rate by 10. Before you take damage from dangerous terrain or falling, reduce that damage by 2 × your tier.

Spend 2 focus to gain 2, which you can only use to make physical skill tests via the following actions: Use a Skill, Gain Advantage, or an action from any Agent talent.

During a conversation or endeavor, at the GM’s discretion, you can instead use both 2 to make an additional contribution for the round.

Agent (Thief) Prerequisite: Thievery 3+; Surefooted talent

When you travel in circles of ill repute, your patron or follower’s name carries remarkable weight.

Before you make a test to interact socially with criminal individuals or groups, you can spend 2 focus to add an Opportunity to the result.

Additionally, when you acquire this talent, gain a utility expertise in Criminal Groups.

Agent (Thief) Prerequisite: Have a patron or follower who is a member of the criminal underworld; Double Down talent
hits.name: Underworld Contacts
hits.shortDescription: Gain Criminal Groups expertise. Spend 2 focus to add an Opportunity to a social test against criminals.

Activation: R

Allies can be an asset or a liability, so you keep an eye on them for their protection and yours.

When a willing ally you can influence within 20 feet of you rolls a plot die, you can use this reaction to use Opportunist on that plot die as if it were your own.

Agent (Investigator) Prerequisite: Deduction 1+; Opportunist key talent
hits.name: Watchful Eye
hits.shortDescription: Use Opportunist on the plot die of a willing ally within 20 feet.

Agent (Investigator) Prerequisite: Deduction 1+; Opportunist key talent
